title: aphyllon parishii subsp. brachylobum
intro: Aphyllon parishii subsp. brachylobum, also known as Orobanche parishii subsp. brachyloba or the lesser broomrape, is a parasitic plant that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Aphyllon parishii subsp. brachylobum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce swelling and fight off infections. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea or used in combination with other herbs to create a tincture or infusion, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy.
